name: HNMS K VIII
events: 9 Sep 1940 HrMs K VIII (Lt. Baron C.W.T. van Boetzelaer, RNN) is taken in hand for a short refit at Surabaya. (1)

17 Sep 1940 HrMs K VIII (Lt. Baron C.W.T. van Boetzelaer, RNN) completed her refit at Surabaya. While running trials she suffered engine defects for which she was to return to Surabaya where she was taken in hand for repairs. (1)

7 Jan 1942 HrMs K VIII is commissioned from reserve with part of the crew of the damaged HrMs K XIII. She now started a period of trials and exercises off Surabaya in which she also served as A/S target on several occasions.
21 Feb 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) was ordered to take up a patrol position to the North of Madura together with HrMs K IX (Lt. J.W. Caspers, RNN).
K VIII however had to return to Surabaya several times for repairs. Her machinery was in a bad state and in need of a refit.
26 Feb 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) is ordered to a patrol position to the South-East of Bawean.
28 Feb 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) is ordered to patrol to the North-West of Surabaya. Despite several serious defects K VIII remained on patrol.
2 Mar 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) arrived at Surabaya to take on board fuel for the escape to Colombo. After fuelling K VIII anchored off Surabaya.
3 Mar 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) departed Surabaya roads for Geraldton, Australia. She had on board 2 naval officers for passage.
6 Mar 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) passed Strait Atlas Southbound.

7 Mar 1942 At 1230 hours (zone -7.5), in position 12°27'N, 115°38'E, HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) sighted three Japanese tankers escorted by two Japanese destroyers. They were however not within attacking range.
12 Mar 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) arrived at Geraldton, Australia.
16 Mar 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) departed Geraldton for Fremantle.
17 Mar 1942 HrMs K VIII (Lt.Cdr. M.A.J. Derksema, RNN) arrived at Fremantle. The old submarine was not used any further because of the many defects she has.
